sources: Eurostat ilc_di01 (equivalised disposable income by decile, PT, 2023)
Eurostat hbs_str_t223 (structure of consumption expenditure by COICOP & income quintile, PT, 2020 wave)
Eurostat hbs_exp_t133 (mean consumption expenditure by income quintile, per adult equivalent)
INE Portugal - Inquérito às Despesas das Famílias
notes: Deciles are annual EQUIVALISED net disposable income in EUR (OECD-modified scale: 1 first adult + 0.5 per extra adult + 0.3 per child). categoryShares are % of total household consumption expenditure remapped from COICOP divisions to the app's expense categories, representing the middle of the distribution. quintileExpenditureMultipliers scale the mean per-adult-equivalent expenditure to each income quintile (grounded in hbs_exp_t133 quintile ratios). Per-quintile category structure is derived in code by applying a documented income gradient (Engel's law) to these shares. avgHouseholdEquivFactor is the OECD-modified equivalence factor of the average national household, used to convert between per-household and per-adult-equivalent figures.
